english: and adj. poor, v. phóns-pa (phons pa); perh. also dejected, disheartened.
= pód-pa (phod pa), Cs.; འཕོན་པོ་ °pón-po ('phon po) = pón-po (phon po); འཕོབ་པ་ °pób-pa ('phob pa) = °bébs-pa ('bebs pa) Sch.

pf. °pyas ('phyas), acc. to Lex. = smód-pa (smod pa) to blame, censure, chide; the context however, in which the word occurs, seems to suggest the meaning: to scoff, to deride, (Sch.) e.g. Dzl. 222, 13. 294, 7. 240, 15; also Pth. mis °pyá-ru °oń (mis 'phya ru 'ong), people will laugh at you.
pf. °pyags ('phyags) or pyag? (phyag) to sweep, to clean Lex., Pth.; cf. pyag-dár (phyag dar).

pf. °pyańs ('phyangs), vb. n. to dpyań-ba (dpyang ba), to hang down, dar sńon-poi ge-ša °pyań-ba (dar sngon po'i ge sha 'phyang ba) a handkerchief of blue silk hanging down from the head Sambh.; má-mču túr-du °pyań-ba (ma mchu tur du 'phyang ba) the lower lip hanging down, as a sign of death S.g.; to cling to a person, from love etc.; rǰe-btsún-gyi skú-la (rje btsun gyi sku la) Mil., to the Reverend's person (or body?); ǰú-žiń °pyań-ba (ju zhing 'phyang ba) to cling to, to take a firm hold of Thgy. - °pyań-tág ('phyang thag) plumb-line, sounding-line C. also dpyań-tag (dpyang thag). - *čuń-kem-pa* (chung kem pa) rope-dancer, esp. at the festivities of new-year C.
Lexx. = yán-pa (yan pa) to ramble, to range, roam about, wander, stray from; °pyán-te °gró-ba ('phyan te 'gro ba) Dzl. 280, 4.

imp. °pyor ('phyor) and pyor (phyor) 1. to raise, to lift up; prú-gu nám-mk'a-la (phru gu nam mkha' la) Glr. to lift the infant up to heaven; to hold aloft, e.g. the dor-ǰe (rdo rje) in practising magic, pointing it towards heaven; so also sdig-mdzúb (sdig mdzub) to raise the finger Mil.; rál-gri (ral gri), to lift up the sword to fetch a blow; to lift up the grain in a shovel, hence: to fan, to sift, to winnow. - 2. to hoist, a flag, frq.; °pyar-dár ('phyar dar) or dar-°pyár (dar 'phyar), a flag; in a general sense: to hang up, so esp. W. *čár-la* (char la) (Lad. *čás-la* (chas la) for čárs-la (chars la)), *bór-če* (bor ce) id.; *čár-la táń-če* (char la tang ce) to hang a man; čár-šiń (char shing) gallows; occasionally too: to cling or stick to an object. - 3. Cs. to show, to represent, to excite, to waken; °pyar-ryeń ('phyar ryeng), engaging, winning behaviour (= °ǰog-sgégs ('jog sgegs)), °pyár-ba byéd-pa ('phyar ba byed pa) to assume an alluring attitude; °pyár-ka-čan ('phyar kha can), tempting, graceful, charming.

I. sbst. marmot, pyí-ba (phyi ba). - II. vb. pf. pyis (phyis), °pyis ('phyis) 1. to be late, to be belated, to come too late; gál-te °pyis-na (gal te 'phyis na), if I come too late Dzl.; da kyod čuń °pyis-pa yin (da khyod cung 'phyis pa yin) you come just a little too late Pth.; °pyí-mo ('phyi mo) v. pyi (phyi) II. - 2. also °pyíd-pa ('phyid pa) to wipe, to blot out, mig (mig) to wipe the eyes Pth.; mčí-ma (mchi ma) the tears Glr.; to pull out, spu (spu) the hair W.; to tear out, rlíg-pa (rlig pa) the testicles Sch.; °pyi-rás ('phyi ras) Cs., wiper, wiping-clout, duster; lág-°pyi (lag 'phyi) Cs., towel, v. pyis (phyis) II.
Sch. to bind, better kyíg-pa (khyig pa).
for °k'yíl-ba ('khyil ba) to wind, to twist, (the hair) Wdn.
rarely čúg-pa (chug pa) to be mistaken, also W.; to miss, lam (lam), the road Lex.; ču-tsód (chu tshod), to mistake the hour Pth.
1. to mount, to rise up, of smoke; to overflow; inundate, of rivers and lakes Lex. - 2. Sch. to heap up, to accumulate? v. pyúr-bu (phyur bu).

pf. °pyes ('phyes), to crawl, to creep, like snakes; esp. lto-°pye (lto 'phye), 'belly-creeper', snake, serpent; °pyé-ba čén-po ('phye ba chen po), name of a demon; °pyé-bo ('phye bo), fem. mo (mo) cripple Lex. = rkań-med (rkang med).
Mil. = pyen (phyen), wind, gtóń-ba (gtong ba), to let go a wind.

pf. °pyos? ('phyos?) 1. to swim, of fishes, Mil. - 2. to soar, to float, in the air Thgy. - 3. to flow, heave, swell, of fluids Mng.; °pyo-dár-ba ('phyo dar ba) Sch., to undulate. - 4. to range, roam about, gambol, rtse-žiń °pyó-ba (rtse zhing 'phyo ba), of deer Mil.; rí-la °pyo (ri la 'phyo)
